description: Fix opentabs start --background silently failing with exit code 1 and zero output on Node.js 24. Two root causes: (1) createWriteStream returns a WriteStream with fd=null before the open event, and Node 24 rejects this in spawn stdio; (2) the global .catch(() => {}) in cli.ts swallows all unexpected errors, producing silent exits.

userStories.title: Wait for WriteStream open before passing to spawn in background mode
userStories.description: As a user running opentabs start --background, I want the server to start successfully so that I can run the server in the background without issues.
acceptanceCriteria: createWriteStream for the log file is awaited until the 'open' event fires (fd is non-null) before being passed to spawn stdio
If the WriteStream fails to open (e.g., permission denied), a clear error message is printed and the process exits with code 1
The spawn call receives a WriteStream with a valid fd, preventing ERR_INVALID_ARG_VALUE on Node.js 24+
Existing foreground start behavior is unchanged (the foreground path uses spawnStreaming with 'pipe' stdio, not WriteStream)

userStories.title: Log unexpected errors in CLI global catch handler instead of swallowing them
userStories.description: As a user running any opentabs command, I want unexpected errors to be printed to stderr so that I can diagnose issues instead of seeing a silent exit with code 1.
acceptanceCriteria: The .catch() handler in cli.ts prints the error message to stderr for unexpected errors
Expected errors (where the action handler already called process.exit or printed an error) do not produce duplicate output
The error message format is: 'Error: <message>' on stderr, matching the style of other CLI error messages
